item.dc:creator: Lawrence Hurley
item.description: The Supreme Court ensured that the abortion pill, mifepristone, can continue to be available by mail without an in-person meeting with a clinician.

media:content.media:text: A hand holds a box of mifepristone
media:content.media:description: Mifepristone is used as part of a two-drug FDA-approved regimen that is now the most common form of abortion in the U.S.

item.title: Supreme Court allows abortion pill to remain available by mail nationwide

item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio tells NBC News' Tom Llamas that President Trump brought up Iran during the China summit and made the U.S. position clear. NBC News correspondents Gabe Gutierrez, Courtney Kube and Andrea Mitchell join Meet the Press NOW discuss Trump's bilateral meeting with Chinese President Xi Jinping. Bonny Lin, director of the China Power Project at the Center for Strategic and International Studies, weighs in on the Taiwan issue hanging over the summit.

media:content.media:title: Marco Rubio says 'we're not asking for China's help' with Iran
media:content.media:description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio tells NBC News' Tom Llamas that President Trump brought up Iran during the China summit and made the U.S. position clear. NBC News correspondents Gabe Gutierrez, Courtney Kube and Andrea Mitchell join Meet the Press NOW discuss Trump's bilateral meeting with Chinese President Xi Jinping. Bonny Lin, director of the China Power Project at the Center for Strategic and International Studies, weighs in on the Taiwan issue hanging over the summit.

item.title: Marco Rubio says 'we're not asking for China's help' with Iran

item.description: President Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping visited the Temple of Heaven after their two-hour bilateral meeting. NBC News’ Garrett Haake reports that, according to Chinese state media, Taiwan was among the most important topics discussed.

media:content.media:title: China warns Taiwan tensions could jeopardize U.S. relationship
media:content.media:description: President Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping visited the Temple of Heaven after their two-hour bilateral meeting. NBC News’ Garrett Haake reports that, according to Chinese state media, Taiwan was among the most important topics discussed.

item.title: China warns Taiwan tensions could jeopardize U.S. relationship

item.dc:creator: Abigail Williams
item.description: After living without their loved ones for more than a decade, two families of Americans detained in China are appealing to President Donald Trump to bring them home as he meets with Chinese leader Xi Jinping in Beijing.

media:content.media:text: Cynthia and Nelson Wells, Sr. with a photo of Nelson, Sr. and their son Nelson Wells, Jr., who’s been imprisoned in China since 2014, in their home in Haughton, La.
media:content.media:description: Cynthia and Nelson Wells Sr. with a photo of their son, Nelson Wells Jr., who’s been imprisoned in China since 2014.
Cynthia and Nelson Wells Sr. with a photo of their son, Nelson Wells Jr., who’s been imprisoned in China since 2014.

media:content.media:text: Dawn Michelle Hunt with her mother and older brother in Chicago in 1991.
media:content.media:description: Cynthia and Nelson Wells Sr. with a photo of their son, Nelson Wells Jr., who’s been imprisoned in China since 2014.

item.title: Families of Americans imprisoned in China appeal to Trump for help

item.description: As the war with Iran pushes its munitions stocks to dangerously low levels, the Defense Department has not signed new contracts to replenish its supplies, according to two U.S. officials and two people familiar with the matter. NBC News' Gordon Lubold details the kinds of weapons and their importance to the Middle East conflict.

media:content.media:title: Sources: Pentagon has no new munitions contracts amid concerns over weapons shortage
media:content.media:description: As the war with Iran pushes its munitions stocks to dangerously low levels, the Defense Department has not signed new contracts to replenish its supplies, according to two U.S. officials and two people familiar with the matter. NBC News' Gordon Lubold details the kinds of weapons and their importance to the Middle East conflict.

item.title: Sources: Pentagon has no new munitions contracts amid concerns over weapons shortage

item.description: In a TODAY exclusive, Alex Murdaugh’s attorneys Dick Harpootlian and Jim Griffin, join TODAY to discuss the South Carolina Supreme Court’s ruling on Wednesday to overturn the double murder convictions and how Murdaugh reacted. "He was very gracious, thankful and he said 'Jim, I'm seeing it, I'm reading it, but it's still hard to believe,'" Griffin says of his conversation with Murduagh. The lawyers also talk about what they expect to be different in the next trial, comment on whether or no

media:content.media:title: How Alex Murdaugh Reacted to Overturning of Murder Convictions
media:content.media:description: In a TODAY exclusive, Alex Murdaugh’s attorneys Dick Harpootlian and Jim Griffin, join TODAY to discuss the South Carolina Supreme Court’s ruling on Wednesday to overturn the double murder convictions and how Murdaugh reacted. "He was very gracious, thankful and he said 'Jim, I'm seeing it, I'm reading it, but it's still hard to believe,'" Griffin says of his conversation with Murduagh. The lawyers also talk about what they expect to be different in the next trial, comment on whether or not Murdaugh will take the stand again, and reveal that their client “will not take a plea deal under any circumstances.”

item.title: How Alex Murdaugh Reacted to Overturning of Murder Convictions

item.dc:creator: Jane C. Timm
item.description: Republicans hope to eliminate a majority-minority seat held by longtime Democratic Rep. Jim Clyburn before the November midterm elections.

media:content.media:description: Republican South Carolina Gov. Henry McMaster had initially decided not to call a special session.

item.title: South Carolina governor calls for a special session on redistricting

item.dc:creator: Evan Bush
Aarne Heikkila
item.description: Since the L.A.-area fires, Altadena residents have found toxic compounds such as lead and asbestos in their living spaces and lots. Many have not returned home.

media:content.media:description: Sixteen months after the Eaton Fire, which torched 9,400 homes in Altadena, Calif., lots remain empty. Residents fear contamination in the soil and inside standing homes that were filled with smoke.
Sixteen months after the Eaton Fire, which torched 9,400 homes in Altadena, Calif., lots remain empty. Residents fear contamination in the soil and inside standing homes that were filled with smoke.
Sixteen months after the Eaton Fire, which torched 9,400 homes in Altadena, Calif., lots remain empty. Residents fear contamination in the soil and inside standing homes that were filled with smoke.

media:content.media:text: Smoke covering Central Park in Pasadena
media:content.media:description: Sixteen months after the Eaton Fire, which torched 9,400 homes in Altadena, Calif., lots remain empty. Residents fear contamination in the soil and inside standing homes that were filled with smoke.
Sixteen months after the Eaton Fire, which torched 9,400 homes in Altadena, Calif., lots remain empty. Residents fear contamination in the soil and inside standing homes that were filled with smoke.
Sixteen months after the Eaton Fire, which torched 9,400 homes in Altadena, Calif., lots remain empty. Residents fear contamination in the soil and inside standing homes that were filled with smoke.
Sixteen months after the Eaton Fire, which torched 9,400 homes in Altadena, Calif., lots remain empty. Residents fear contamination in the soil and inside standing homes that were filled with smoke.
Sixteen months after the Eaton Fire, which torched 9,400 homes in Altadena, Calif., lots remain empty. Residents fear contamination in the soil and inside standing homes that were filled with smoke.
Sixteen months after the Eaton Fire, which torched 9,400 homes in Altadena, Calif., lots remain empty. Residents fear contamination in the soil and inside standing homes that were filled with smoke.
Sixteen months after the Eaton Fire, which torched 9,400 homes in Altadena, Calif., lots remain empty. Residents fear contamination in the soil and inside standing homes that were filled with smoke.
Sixteen months after the Eaton Fire, which torched 9,400 homes in Altadena, Calif., lots remain empty. Residents fear contamination in the soil and inside standing homes that were filled with smoke.
Sixteen months after the Eaton Fire, which torched 9,400 homes in Altadena, Calif., lots remain empty. Residents fear contamination in the soil and inside standing homes that were filled with smoke.
Sixteen months after the Eaton Fire, which torched 9,400 homes in Altadena, Calif., lots remain empty. Residents fear contamination in the soil and inside standing homes that were filled with smoke.
Sixteen months after the Eaton Fire, which torched 9,400 homes in Altadena, Calif., lots remain empty. Residents fear contamination in the soil and inside standing homes that were filled with smoke.

item.title: The toxic aftermath of the L.A.-area fires

item.dc:creator: Rob Wile
item.description: After an initial furor over jaw-dropping ticket prices, global soccer fans may be starting to see some relief — at least in the resale market.

media:content.media:text: A FIFA World Cup ball on the pitch at Arrowhead Stadium on May 11, 2026 in Kansas City, Mo.
media:content.media:description: A FIFA World Cup ball on the pitch at Arrowhead Stadium in Kansas City, Mo.

media:content.media:text: FIFA World Cup Trophy Tour in Houston
media:content.media:description: A FIFA World Cup ball on the pitch at Arrowhead Stadium in Kansas City, Mo.

item.title: Why the prices of World Cup resale tickets are starting to drop

item.description: As album rollouts continue to evolve, fans recently attempted to melt a giant ice block in the middle of Toronto as part of Drake's "Iceman" announcement. But how did we get here? NBC News' Gadi Schwartz takes a look back at other memorable rollouts, and how they have advanced through the years.

media:content.media:title: The evolution of album rollouts
media:content.media:description: As album rollouts continue to evolve, fans recently attempted to melt a giant ice block in the middle of Toronto as part of Drake's "Iceman" announcement. But how did we get here? NBC News' Gadi Schwartz takes a look back at other memorable rollouts, and how they have advanced through the years.

item.title: The evolution of album rollouts

item.dc:creator: Jane C. Timm
item.description: Republicans hope to eliminate a majority-minority seat held by longtime Democratic Rep. Jim Clyburn before the November midterm elections.

media:content.media:description: Republican South Carolina Gov. Henry McMaster had initially decided not to call a special session.

item.title: South Carolina governor calls for a special session on redistricting

item.dc:creator: Meriam Bouarrouj
Doha Madani
item.description: Two people were killed when a plane crashed into a residence in Akron, Ohio, on Thursday afternoon, local and federal authorities said
item.title: 2 dead after plane crashes into Ohio home

item.dc:creator: Lawrence Hurley
item.description: The Supreme Court ensured that the abortion pill, mifepristone, can continue to be available by mail without an in-person meeting with a clinician.

media:content.media:text: A hand holds a box of mifepristone
media:content.media:description: Mifepristone is used as part of a two-drug FDA-approved regimen that is now the most common form of abortion in the U.S.

item.title: Supreme Court allows abortion pill to remain available by mail nationwide

item.dc:creator: The Politics Desk
item.description: In today’s edition, Jonathan Allen explores the delicate balance Republicans are attempting to strike for the midterms

media:content.media:description: Republicans are debating what President Donald Trump's role should be on the campaign trail in the midterms .

item.title: Trump presents a midterm dilemma for Republicans: From the Politics Desk

item.dc:creator: Alicia Victoria Lozano
Doha Madani
item.description: The resignation of a California mayor who pleaded guilty to acting as a foreign agent for China has sparked backlash and reignited fears of anti-Asian discrimination.

media:content.media:text: Eileen Wang speaks at City Hall in Arcadia, Calif., in 2025.
media:content.media:description: Eileen Wang speaks at City Hall in Arcadia, Calif., in 2025.

item.title: Racial backlash erupts online after California mayor admits to being an agent for China

item.dc:creator: Alicia Victoria Lozano
item.description: Six people whose bodies were found in a boxcar in Texas died from heatstroke before law enforcement found them, Laredo’s mayor said.

media:content.media:text: Chief Miguel A. Rodriguez Jr., center, speaks
media:content.media:description: A Union Pacific sign at a rail yard in Laredo, Texas, on Sunday.
A Union Pacific sign at a rail yard in Laredo, Texas, on Sunday.

item.title: Police say 6 migrants found in train car died of heatstroke

item.dc:creator: Corky Siemaszko
item.description: A fugitive from Detroit recently arrested for killing his punk rock singer roommate back in 1993 has been described in news reports as a “ghost” because he was able to elude the authorities for more than 30 years.

media:content.media:text: A U.S. Marshal walks with Richard Werstine as he is returned to the U.S.
media:content.media:description: A U.S. Marshal walks with Richard Werstine as he is returned to the U.S.

item.title: Detroit cold case murder suspect arrested after more than 30 years on the lam

item.dc:creator: David Ingram
item.description: Elon Musk had traveled to China with President Donald Trump despite the judge in the case saying he was not excused.

media:content.media:text: Photos of Elon Musk and Sam Altman in elevators at federal court in Oakland, Calif.
media:content.media:description: Elon Musk and Sam Altman in elevators at federal court in Oakland, Calif.

item.title: Musk’s attorney apologizes for his absence at trial during closing arguments

item.dc:creator: Francie Ebert
item.description: On Thursday, Dylan Sprouse and his wife, Barbara Palvin, announced their pregnancy while attending the 79th Annual Cannes Film Festival in Cannes, France

media:content.media:text: Dylan Sprouse and Barbara Palvin at the Cannes Film Festival on May 14, 2026 in Cannes, France.
media:content.media:description: Dylan Sprouse and Barbara Palvin at the Cannes Film Festival on Thursday.

item.title: Barbara Palvin and Dylan Sprouse are expecting their first child

item.dc:creator: Rohan Nadkarni
item.description: In the Eastern Conference semifinals, the Detroit Pistons seemed in control in a pivotal Game 5 last night, until the Cleveland Cavaliers stormed back to win in overtime.

media:content.media:description: Cleveland Cavaliers guard James Harden shoots on Detroit Pistons guard Caris LeVert in the first half during game five of the second round of the 2026 NBA Playoffs at Little Caesars Arena.
Cleveland Cavaliers guard James Harden shoots on Detroit Pistons guard Caris LeVert in the first half during game five of the second round of the 2026 NBA Playoffs at Little Caesars Arena.
Cleveland Cavaliers guard James Harden shoots on Detroit Pistons guard Caris LeVert in the first half during game five of the second round of the 2026 NBA Playoffs at Little Caesars Arena.
Cleveland Cavaliers guard James Harden shoots on Detroit Pistons guard Caris LeVert in the first half during game five of the second round of the 2026 NBA Playoffs at Little Caesars Arena.

item.title: From The Sports Desk: Cleveland rocks

item.dc:creator: Doha Madani
item.description: Human remains have been found at a Texas home as part of an investigation into the disappearance of a 6-year-old boy who hasn’t been seen in nearly four years.

media:content.media:description: Noel Rodriguez-Alvarez.

item.title: Human remains discovered in search for missing 6-year-old

item.dc:creator: Daniella Silva
The Associated Press
item.description: U.S. Border Patrol Chief Michael Banks’ departure is the latest leadership shake-up of officials implementing President Donald Trump’s immigration crackdown

media:content.media:description: Michael Banks in Nogales, Ariz., on Feb. 4.

item.title: Border Patrol chief resigns in latest DHS leadership shake-up

item.dc:creator: Doha Madani
item.description: It might be the smallest U.S. city to host the FIFA World Cup this summer, but Kansas City organizers are hoping to be considered the mightiest by fans who will be arriving in the Midwest to celebrate the biggest single-sport tournament in the world.

media:content.media:text: A general view of Arrowhead Stadium
media:content.media:description: Arrowhead Stadium in Kansas City, Mo., on Monday ahead of the World Cup.

media:content.media:text: A FIFA World Cup ball on the pitch at Arrowhead Stadium on May 11, 2026 in Kansas City, Mo.
media:content.media:description: Arrowhead Stadium in Kansas City, Mo., on Monday ahead of the World Cup.

item.title: Kansas City used ‘Midwestern pioneering spirit’ to host World Cup games, teams

item.dc:creator: Matt Lavietes
item.description: A plane carrying 11 people crashed off the coast of Melbourne, Florida, the U.S. Coast Guard announced Wednesday.

media:content.media:credit: via U.S. Air Force Reserve Command
media:content.media:description: A U.S. Air Force HC-130J Combat King II, HH-60W Jolly Green II helicopter and Pararescuemen assigned to the 920th Rescue Wing conduct rescue operations for survivors of a downed civilian aircraft off the coast of Melbourne, Fla., May 12, 2026.

media:content.media:credit: via U.S. Air Force Reserve Command
media:content.media:description: An emergency locator transmitter signal alerted authorities around 11 a.m. ET, officials said.

item.title: 11 people survive plane crash off Florida coast

item.dc:creator: Henry J. Gomez
item.description: Vice President JD Vance offered a vote of confidence Thursday for Sen. Sen. Susan Collins, a Republican who faces a tough re-election fight this year in Maine.

media:content.media:description: JD Vance delivers remarks at Bangor International Airport May 14, 2026 in Bangor, Maine.

item.title: Vance calls Sen. Susan Collins ‘a good fit for Maine’ despite his frustrations with her
